records.ety: [L. occurrere, occursum; ob (see Ob-) + currere to run. See Course.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Occurred(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Occurring (?).]

quotes: The resistance of the bodies they occur with.

defs: To go in order to meet; to make reply.
quotes: I must occur to one specious objection.

defs: To meet one's eye; to be found or met with; to present itself; to appear.
quotes: In Scripture, though the word heir occur, yet there is no such thing as “heir” in our author's sense.

defs: To happen; to take place; as, I will write if opportunity occurs.
To come to the mind; to arise in the mind; to suggest itself; to be presented to the imagination or memory; -- of an idea or thought; as, it never occurred to me to call John to ask.
quotes: There doth not occur to me any use of this experiment for profit.
